title: Pungent Pan-Seared Russian Beef Casserole
description: A show-off Russian dish featuring a rich mixture of pungent flavors, tender katsuramuki-cut beef, and a hint of orange blossom water, all carefully prepared in a copper pot to create a gooey, savory masterpiece.
instructions: Heat a copper pot over medium-high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of oil and sear the beef strips until browned, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the beef from the pot and set it aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the remaining oil.
Sauté the mushrooms and onions until they are softened and fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Sprinkle the all-purpose flour over the vegetables and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually pour in the beef broth, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the orange blossom water (if using) and heavy cream.
Return the beef to the pot and simmer for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the beef is cooked to your liking.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the pungent pan-seared Russian beef casserole hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
